pattern: Counting each value's multiplicity and testing whether a common group size above one divides them all reduces to the greatest common divisor of the counts.
hints: Group identical cards first and look only at how many copies each distinct value has.
A single group size must divide every one of those counts, so any common divisor greater than one works.
Take the greatest common divisor of all the per-value counts; the partition is possible exactly when that gcd is at least two.

edge_inputs.description: pairs of every value can be split into groups of two
counts three and two share no common divisor above one
single card cannot form any group larger than one
two equal cards form one group of two
two distinct singletons cannot be grouped
counts four and two share divisor two
counts six and three share divisor three
one value appears a prime number of times unmatched by others

stress.description: random small-value cards so several values repeat with varied counts
stress.note: narrow value range forces many repeats, exercising the gcd reduction

stress.description: construct counts that all share a common divisor so the answer is true

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def hasGroupsSizeX(self, deck):
        from collections import Counter
        from math import gcd
        from functools import reduce
        counts = list(Counter(deck).values())
        return reduce(gcd, counts) >= 2
